findings.summary: Codex readiness is overstated because docs require codex_hooks plus hooks.json, but the checked-in config omits codex_hooks and the detector still returns live from .codex/settings.json alone.
findings.recommended_fix: Make the checked-in Codex contract and the detector agree: either add the missing activation prerequisites or tighten detectCodexHookPolicy so live requires the documented feature flag and hook registration.

findings.summary: Copilot wrapper parity is still reverted: the live .claude/settings.local.json lacks the top-level wrapper fields and writer commands that packets 010 and 011 say must be reapplied.
findings.recommended_fix: Reapply packets 010 and 011 in .claude/settings.local.json and add a regression test that asserts the top-level Copilot wrapper fields and writer commands remain present.

findings.summary: Gemini's live hook surface uses BeforeAgent, PreCompress, and SessionEnd, but docs and fallback tests still describe older or Claude-shaped names and availability assumptions.
findings.recommended_fix: Align Gemini docs and runtime fixtures to the checked-in event names, or introduce a single normalization layer that both docs and tests consume.

findings.summary: Copilot readiness detection keys off generic .github hook files, so unrelated Copilot hooks such as Superset can make Spec Kit parity appear enabled.
findings.recommended_fix: Detect Spec Kit Copilot readiness from .claude/settings.local.json wrapper presence, not from any .github/hooks sessionStart file.

findings.summary: Startup parity is still uneven: Claude and Codex thread cached-summary and spec-folder-aware continuity into startup context, while Gemini and Copilot use thinner startup builders.
findings.recommended_fix: Refactor a shared startup-state adapter so Gemini and Copilot can pass the same continuity inputs as Claude, even if their transport output stays runtime-specific.

findings.summary: advisor_status conflates daemon availability with freshness readability, so operators can read daemonAvailable as true even when no daemon PID or process evidence is present.
findings.recommended_fix: Split freshness from daemon process presence in advisor_status and back daemon-running reporting with a real PID/lease/heartbeat source.
